settings.title: Rooted in Guatemala
settings.text: Finca El Rosario Quesada — one of the oldest plantation homes in the region with over 200 years of history, near the ancient Mayan park Takalik Abaj.

settings.title: 7 Cacao Varieties
settings.text: We cultivate seven different cacao varieties on the same land, allowing for biodiversity, resilience, and a layered flavor spectrum unique to our farm.

settings.title: Zero Waste Process
settings.text: Nothing is wasted. Leftover organic material is returned to the land as natural fertilizer, closing the cycle and nourishing future harvests.

settings.title: Community Rooted
settings.text: Our team comes from the local community — many are direct descendants of Mayan heritage, working in alignment with ancestral agricultural traditions.

settings.title: Hand Germination
settings.text: Each plant is germinated by hand with care and intention.

settings.title: Individual Monitoring
settings.text: Trees are grown and monitored individually throughout their lifecycle.

settings.title: Hand-Harvested
settings.text: Pods are hand-harvested at peak ripeness to ensure optimal flavor.

settings.title: Manual Sorting
settings.text: Fruit is opened and sorted manually, ensuring only the best beans proceed.

settings.title: Custom Fermentation
settings.text: Beans are processed using custom fermentation protocols refined through generations.

settings.intro_title: Born in the Rainforest of Guatemala
settings.intro_rte:
Cacao Senses is born in the tropical rainforest of Guatemala, deep in ancestral land located six hours from the city, where nature remains untouched and traditions remain alive. Our farm, Finca El Rosario Quesada, is one of the oldest plantation homes in the region, with more than 200 years of history.

We are located in the vicinity of the ancient Mayan archaeological park Takalik Abaj — one of the earliest and most important ceremonial and trade centers of Mesoamerica.
